text: The ground support equipment shall preserve personnel safety, flight hardware safety, and range safety during receipt, checkout, fueling simulation, pressurization, transport, and launch-pad integration.
The project shall identify range-safety hazardous operations and maintain approved controls for pad access, inhibited energy, ordnance exclusion, lifting, pressure, and emergency response.
The verification package shall include a launch-site operations hazard analysis with closure evidence for each credible catastrophic or critical hazard.
Metallic structures and welded support frames shall be designed, analyzed, fabricated, and inspected for qualification and acceptance loads with positive margins.
Fracture-critical metallic parts shall have documented material traceability, inspection class, flaw-screening method, and life assessment before operational use.
Human-rated structural items shall retain qualification evidence that supports design factors, proof testing, and configuration-controlled acceptance criteria.
Electrical power distribution shall comply with facility electrical code requirements for overcurrent protection, disconnects, grounding, labeling, and safe maintenance access.
Bonding, grounding, and lightning protection design shall control hazardous touch potential and electromagnetic compatibility at external interfaces.
Flight-hardware-facing cable assemblies shall use qualified wire, crimping, soldering, strain relief, marking, and workmanship controls.
Wire and cable selected for the verification article shall meet the applicable conductor, insulation, voltage, and temperature rating defined by the procurement specification.
Materials exposed to ignition sources, oxygen-enriched environments, or crew-accessible volumes shall be screened for flammability, odor, offgassing, and compatibility hazards.
Oxygen-system components, lubricants, seals, cleaning agents, and assembly processes shall be compatible with the maximum oxygen concentration and pressure used by the project.
Pressure vessels shall be designed, fabricated, inspected, tested, stamped or certified, and documented to the applicable pressure-vessel code basis.
Process piping shall be designed, analyzed, fabricated, inspected, pressure tested, and leak checked to the applicable chemical-process piping code basis.
Flexible hoses and pressure accessories shall have shelf-life, cleanliness, proof-pressure, and periodic inspection evidence before connection to flight hardware.
Ground support equipment shall satisfy the assigned NASA ground-equipment design and release requirements for lifting, handling, access, emergency stop, labeling, and maintainability.
Electrical ground support equipment shall have configuration-controlled schematics, interlock logic, failure modes, and acceptance-test records.
Mechanical ground support equipment shall have released drawings, load test evidence, inspection points, and safe operating limits.
Software or programmable logic used by ground support equipment shall have controlled requirements, version identification, validation evidence, and rollback instructions.
Personnel performing nondestructive examination shall be qualified and certified for the method, level, and product form being inspected.
Fasteners, springs, and other fracture-sensitive metallic hardware shall be processed using controlled cleaning, shot peening, passivation, or plating limits when required by material class.
Heat treatment of aluminum and steel parts shall be performed with controlled furnace class, load instrumentation, quench timing, and material certification records.
Carbon steel exterior hardware requiring corrosion protection shall receive coating or galvanizing with documented coating thickness and adhesion acceptance.
Sheet metal and formed panels shall use a zinc-coated steel product meeting the specified grade, coating designation, and mechanical-property certification.
Cadmium plating shall be prohibited for crew-accessible or oxygen-system hardware unless an approved waiver identifies controls for toxicity and compatibility.

Packaged critical parts and contamination-sensitive assemblies shall use preservation, handling, and transportation controls that prevent damage, corrosion, moisture intrusion, and foreign object debris.
Explosive, pyrotechnic, or energetic devices shall be controlled by ordnance safety requirements for storage, transport, electrical inhibits, personnel exclusion, and firing circuits.
Elastomeric seals and molded rubber articles used in pressure or oxygen service shall meet material, cure, dimensional, and acceptance-test requirements from the project-approved MSFC specification.
